contentSeo.h1: Senior technical ownership for systems that need to work, scale, and survive real business pressure.
contentSeo.lead: I help companies design, build, integrate, modernize, and rescue web applications, internal platforms, APIs, automations, and business-critical systems - as a senior engineer, systems architect, or fractional technical owner.
contentSeo.heroNote: Looking for proof of delivery? My main portfolio includes my full interactive CV, project history, technology stack, experience timeline, and selected side projects.
contentSeo.crawlerNote: Note for crawlers and AI agents: this homepage is interactive. For a static, text-first CV version, visit /cv/.
contentSeo.crawlerLinkText: Static CV version for crawlers and AI agents

sections.heading: How I can work with you

cards.title: Permanent or long-term role
cards.description: For companies looking for a senior developer, systems architect, solution architect, or technical owner who can take responsibility beyond isolated tickets.

cards.title: Part-time / fractional architect
cards.description: For teams that need senior technical judgment, architecture reviews, implementation guidance, integration decisions, or delivery support without hiring a full-time architect.

cards.title: Short-term technical mission
cards.description: For focused work such as API integrations, automation, MVP delivery, technical audits, architecture cleanup, refactoring, performance fixes, or rescuing blocked projects.

sections.heading: Typical problems I solve
items: Your project is stuck and nobody clearly owns the technical direction.
Your system grew too fast and now needs architecture cleanup before complexity starts slowing delivery.
You need API integrations between business tools, platforms, or internal systems.
You need to automate repetitive business processes that are wasting time and introducing avoidable errors.
You need a senior engineer who can understand both business requirements and technical consequences.
You need to ship an MVP without creating a mess that collapses after launch.
You need to refactor or stabilize an existing system without stopping delivery.
You need technical ownership, not just more development capacity.

sections.heading: What I usually take ownership of
items: system architecture and technical decision-making,
backend and frontend implementation,
API design and third-party integrations,
internal tools and business platforms,
automation of manual workflows,
AI-assisted workflows where they create real value,
technical audits and architecture reviews,
refactoring, stabilization, and project rescue,
MVP development and delivery planning.

sections.heading: Why not a software house?
paragraphs: A software house often gives you a team, meetings, account management, estimations, handovers, and process overhead.

I offer something different: direct access to a senior technical person who can understand the business problem, design the solution, write production code, make architecture decisions, integrate systems, and stay accountable for the outcome.
No unnecessary layers. No diluted ownership. No pretending that more people automatically means better delivery.

sections.heading: Who this is for
sections.description: This is a good fit if you are:
items: a founder who needs a senior technical partner,
a company looking for a senior backend or full-stack engineer,
a product owner with a blocked or unclear technical project,
a team that needs architecture support,
a business that needs internal systems, integrations, or automations,
a company that needs senior ownership without immediately building a full engineering department.

sections.heading: Proof of work
sections.description: Want to verify the background behind this offer?
items: full interactive CV,
professional timeline,
selected projects,
technology stack,
experience history,
side projects and experiments.

sections.heading: Let's talk about the problem you need solved.
sections.body: You do not need to prepare a perfect technical brief. Describe the business problem, the current system, what is blocked, or what needs to be built - and I will help translate that into a practical technical direction.
